key_facts: `pyproject.toml` configures pytest to discover `tests`, `test_*.py`, `Test*`, and `test_*`, with verbose strict markers and short tracebacks.
The Python test matrix includes root tests, 35 integration tests, 37 Codex-team tests, 16 hook tests, 9 orchestration tests, 7 execution tests, and 6 contract tests in the current inventory.
`PYTHONPATH=src; python -m specify_cli testing inventory --format json` detects one healthy Python root module and Rust workspace/crate modules under the engine.
Template/project-map tests assert that map-scan/map-build define scan package, packet evidence, reverse coverage, and layered atlas contracts.
Engine `package.json` exposes `npm run build`, `npm run start:runtime`, `npm run test:bridge`, and `npm run test:native-hooks`; the Rust workspace exposes cargo-testable crates.
Caches, virtualenvs, dist outputs, temporary smoke outputs, and worktrees are inventory-only unless a specific failure points at them.
recommended_atlas_updates: Document verification commands by change surface instead of only listing `pytest -q`.
Document source-aware CLI invocation for development checks.
Document excluded local output buckets and revisit rules.
unknowns: Engine Rust test execution was mapped from manifests and file layout; full Rust test run is separate from Python pytest.
